html:
Powdercake is a Food item cooked in a Crock Pot by combining 1 Corn, 1 Honey, and 1 Twig. It takes 10 seconds to cook. It takes the longest time to rot out of all foods and was implemented as a joke.

Adding any Fishes may result in Fishsticks or Fish Tacos, while adding a Mandrake may result in Mandrake Soup.

Powdercake restores 0 , 0 , and -3 upon consumption. It takes 18750 days to spoil.


Prerequisites

  • Requires:
  • Requires: 
  • Requires:

Recipe

Examples

×2

33% chance to result in Fishsticks
33% chance to result in Fish Tacos
50% chance to result in Mandrake Soup.

Usage

Tips

  • It serves well as an everlasting bait for the Incredible Inedible Farm, where mobs such as Pigs, Gobblers or Merms can be lured into Tooth Traps or a walled off area.
  • In Don't Starve Together, Powdercakes are ideal for conserving seasoning effects, thanks to their extremely long conservation time. The player can season them, choose a different character as Warly, and still be able to benefit from the effects of seasoned food.
    • This method can be an alternative way to conserve seasoned food with Bundling Wraps.

Trivia

  • Powdercakes were added to the game as a prank, since the developers used it as an inside joke around their office.
  • The Powdercake is very similar to the Twinkie, which went out of production shortly before the update that included Powdercakes. There is an urban legend that claims Twinkies will never spoil. Though they are stated to have a shelf life of 25 days, they will stay intact without rotting or losing too much flavor for over 30 years. They do grow more stale and less firm over time, however, due to oxidation and water vapor diffusion through the plastic wrapper.
  • Before the full Caves release, Powdercakes were originally called Powcakes. This was a reference to Riptidepow, a popular Twitch user who sometimes played Don't Starve and was mentioned by a developer in an interview during an exclusive first look at Caves.
  • It takes at least 2,500 hours (just over 104 real life days) of gameplay to rot a Powdercake (not counting Winter and Summer rot multipliers, or time spent with map open). It is thus unrealistic that anyone would see a Powdercake rot without commands or access to tools that allow them to pass time at an unnatural rate. Even then it would not be worth it, due to the result only being one piece of rot.

Gallery

  • Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord

–Warly

–Wilba

–Wormwood
“Looks good enough to fire through the air.”
–Wheeler
“Gotta eat what you can around here.”

–Wortox
“Gives tummy-ache, but... smell so good...”
–Wurt
“This could go badly.”
–Walter
“This never ends well.”
–Wanda

Adding any Fishes may result in Fishsticks or Fish Tacos, while adding a Mandrake may result in Mandrake Soup.
Powdercake restores 0 , 0 , and -3 upon consumption. It takes 18750 days to spoil.

It serves well as an everlasting bait for the Incredible Inedible Farm, where mobs such as Pigs, Gobblers or Merms can be lured into Tooth Traps or a walled off area.
In Don't Starve Together, Powdercakes are ideal for conserving seasoning effects, thanks to their extremely long conservation time. The player can season them, choose a different character as Warly, and still be able to benefit from the effects of seasoned food.
This method can be an alternative way to conserve seasoned food with Bundling Wraps.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Powdercakes were added to the game as a prank, since the developers used it as an inside joke around their office.
The Powdercake is very similar to the Twinkie, which went out of production shortly before the update that included Powdercakes. There is an urban legend that claims Twinkies will never spoil. Though they are stated to have a shelf life of 25 days, they will stay intact without rotting or losing too much flavor for over 30 years. They do grow more stale and less firm over time, however, due to oxidation and water vapor diffusion through the plastic wrapper.
Before the full Caves release, Powdercakes were originally called Powcakes. This was a reference to Riptidepow, a popular Twitch user who sometimes played Don't Starve and was mentioned by a developer in an interview during an exclusive first look at Caves.
It takes at least 2,500 hours (just over 104 real life days) of gameplay to rot a Powdercake (not counting Winter and Summer rot multipliers, or time spent with map open). It is thus unrealistic that anyone would see a Powdercake rot without commands or access to tools that allow them to pass time at an unnatural rate. Even then it would not be worth it, due to the result only being one piece of rot.

summaryViHtml:
Tóm tắt

Bánh Bột là một Thực Phẩm được nấu trong Nồi Hầm bằng cách kết hợp 1 Bắp, 1 Mật Ong và 1 Cành Cây. Phải mất 10 giây để nấu ăn. Phải mất nhiều thời gian nhất để phân hủy tất cả các loại thực phẩm và được thực hiện như một trò đùa.

The player can season them, choose a different character as Warly, and still be able to benefit from the effects of seasoned food. **This method can be an alternative way to conserve seasoned food with [[Bundling Wrap|Bundling Wraps]]. == {{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Powdercakes were added to the game as a prank, since the developers used it as an inside joke around their office. * The Powdercake is very similar to the [[Wikipedia:Twinkie|Twinkie]], which went out of production shortly before the [[Version History#Underground|update]] that included Powdercakes. There is an urban legend that claims Twinkies will never spoil. Though they are stated to have a shelf life of 25 days, they will stay intact without rotting or losing too much flavor for over 30 years. They do grow more stale and less firm over time, however, due to oxidation and water vapor diffusion through the plastic wrapper. * Before the full [[Caves]] release, Powdercakes were originally called Powcakes. This was a reference to [http://www.twitch.tv/riptidepow Riptidepow], a popular Twitch user who sometimes played ''[[Don't Starve]]'' and was mentioned by a developer in an interview during an exclusive first look at Caves. * It takes at least 2,500 hours (just over 104 real life days) of gameplay to rot a Powdercake (not counting [[Seasons/Winter|Winter]] and [[Seasons/Summer|Summer]] rot multipliers, or time spent with map open). It is thus unrealistic that anyone would see a Powdercake rot without [[Console/Commands|commands]] or access to tools that allow them to pass time at an unnatural rate. Even then it would not be worth it, due to the result only being one piece of [[rot]]. == {{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery == Powdercake 128x128.png|Icon used as emoji for Official Klei Discord {{Crock Pot Food}} [[fr:Cake de poudre]] [[vi:Powdercake]] [[zh:粉末蛋糕]] [[Category:Crock Pot Recipes]] [[Category:Vegetables]] [[Category:Flammable Objects]] [[Category:Perishables]] [[Category:Health Loss]]
